hawkish_phrases: does not expect it will be appropriate to reduce the target range until it has gained greater confidence that inflation is moving sustainably toward 2 percent
remained highly attentive to inflation risks
risks of moving too quickly to ease the stance of policy
upside risks to inflation as having diminished but noted that inflation was still above the Committee's longer-run goal
risk that progress toward price stability could stall
dovish_phrases: judged that the policy rate was likely at its peak for this tightening cycle
risks to achieving its employment and inflation goals are moving into better balance
Inflation has eased over the past year
downside risks to the economy associated with maintaining an overly restrictive stance for too long
core PCE inflation was just below 2 percent on a 6-month basis
summary: The Committee unanimously held rates at 5.25-5.5 percent, acknowledged the policy rate is likely at its peak, but stated it does not expect cuts will be appropriate until gaining greater confidence inflation is moving sustainably toward 2 percent, balancing recognition of easing inflation with persistent caution on inflation risks.
